text: Standing among curious minds and technological wonders, I feel a sense of fascination. The robot—Pepper—stands as both a marvel and an enigma: familiar in form, yet unfamiliar in its expressionless face. The admiration for its design sparks reflection. What does beauty mean if it is stripped of the signals we usually connect to emotion and intent? My own feelings drift between intrigue and a slight unease, pondering what we seek from the faces of others—robotic or human. The presence of others, their conversation and enthusiasm, draws me in, but I feel quietly separate, watching, analyzing, marveling at how we project meaning onto objects and one another.
